message.content:
Integrate Stripe subscriptions into MindWell. Here's everything needed:
Database: Add a migration file supabase/migrations/007_add_subscription.sql that adds a subscription_status column (text, default 'free') and stripe_customer_id column (text, nullable) to the users/profiles table. Also add an is_comped boolean column defaulting to false. A user has Pro access if subscription_status = 'pro' OR is_comped = true.
API routes to create:

POST /api/stripe/checkout — creates a Stripe Checkout session for the logged-in user and returns the URL
POST /api/stripe/webhook — handles checkout.session.completed, customer.subscription.updated, customer.subscription.deleted events and updates subscription_status in Supabase accordingly
GET /api/stripe/portal — creates a Stripe Customer Portal session so users can manage their subscription

Pricing page: Create app/pricing/page.tsx — a clean, on-brand page showing Free vs Pro tiers. Free tier: up to 3 active items. Pro: unlimited items, full PDF export, tag search. Pro pricing: $6/month or $45/year. Include a Subscribe button that calls the checkout API and redirects to Stripe. Match MindWell aesthetic.
Free tier enforcement: In the main home page and add item modal, check subscription status on load. If the user is free and already has 3 or more active items, disable the Add button and show a small upgrade prompt linking to /pricing.
Environment variables needed: STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, STRIPE_MONTHLY_PRICE_ID, STRIPE_ANNUAL_PRICE_ID.
Write files in two parts if any single file exceeds 150 lines — write lines 1-150, stop, wait for confirmation. Push to GitHub when all files are done.

1→-- ============================================================
     2→-- Reading Tracker – Supabase Schema
     3→-- Run this in the Supabase SQL editor to set up your database.
     4→-- ============================================================
     5→
     6→-- Books
     7→CREATE TABLE books (
     8→  id               UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
     9→  title            TEXT NOT NULL,
    10→  current_page     INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    11→  total_pages      INTEGER NOT NULL,
    12→  sessions_per_week INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 3,
    13→  status           TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active'
    14→                     CHECK (status IN ('active', 'completed', 'paused', 'queued')),
    15→  is_priority      BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    16→  created_at       TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    17→  updated_at       TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
    18→);
    19→
    20→-- Sessions
    21→CREATE TABLE sessions (
    22→  id               UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    23→  book_id          UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES books(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    24→  scheduled_date   DATE NOT NULL,
    25→  pages_start      INTEGER NOT NULL,
    26→  pages_end        INTEGER NOT NULL,
    27→  actual_end_page  INTEGER,           -- set when user logs actual progress
    28→  is_completed     BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    29→  order_index      INTEGER NOT NULL,  -- position in the book's schedule
    30→  created_at       TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    31→  updated_at       TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
    32→);
    33→
    34→-- Priority items (articles, one-offs that displace reading sessions)
    35→CREATE TABLE priority_items (
    36→  id               UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    37→  title            TEXT NOT NULL,
    38→  description      TEXT,
    39→  sessions_needed  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    40→  scheduled_date   DATE NOT NULL,
    41→  is_completed     BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    42→  created_at       TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
    43→);
    44→
    45→-- Per-book notes for Outline and Scratch tabs (one row per book per tab)
    46→CREATE TABLE book_notes (
    47→  id               UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    48→  book_id          UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES books(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    49→  tab              TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (tab IN ('outline', 'scratch')),
    50→  content          TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    51→  updated_at       TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    52→  UNIQUE (book_id, tab)
    53→);
    54→
    55→-- Quotes (separate rows so they can be listed individually)
    56→CREATE TABLE quotes (
    57→  id               UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    58→  book_id          UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES books(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    59→  quote_text       TEXT NOT NULL,
    60→  page_number      INTEGER,
    61→  created_at       TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
    62→);
    63→
    64→-- ── Indexes ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
    65→
    66→CREATE INDEX sessions_book_id_idx        ON sessions(book_id);
    67→CREATE INDEX sessions_scheduled_date_idx ON sessions(scheduled_date);
    68→CREATE INDEX priority_items_date_idx     ON priority_items(scheduled_date);
    69→CREATE INDEX book_notes_book_id_idx      ON book_notes(book_id);
    70→CREATE INDEX quotes_book_id_idx          ON quotes(book_id);
    71→
    72→-- ── updated_at trigger ───────────────────────────────────────
    73→
    74→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_updated_at()
    75→RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
    76→BEGIN
    77→  NEW.updated_at = NOW();
    78→  RETURN NEW;
    79→END;
    80→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
    81→
    82→CREATE TRIGGER books_updated_at
    83→  BEFORE UPDATE ON books
    84→  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at();
    85→
    86→CREATE TRIGGER sessions_updated_at
    87→  BEFORE UPDATE ON sessions
    88→  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at();
    89→
    90→CREATE TRIGGER book_notes_updated_at
    91→  BEFORE UPDATE ON book_notes
    92→  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at();
    93→
    94→-- ── Row Level Security (optional but recommended) ────────────
    95→-- Enable RLS and allow all access for now (single-user app).
    96→-- Replace the policies with auth-based ones when you add login.
    97→
    98→ALTER TABLE books          ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
    99→ALTER TABLE sessions       ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
   100→ALTER TABLE priority_items ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
   101→ALTER TABLE book_notes     ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
   102→ALTER TABLE quotes         ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
   103→
   104→CREATE POLICY "allow all" ON books          FOR ALL USING (true) WITH CHECK (true);

   107→CREATE POLICY "allow all" ON book_notes     FOR ALL USING (true) WITH CHECK (true);

1→-- Migration 005: User-scoped RLS + claim function
     2→-- Run in Supabase SQL editor AFTER configuring Google OAuth in Auth settings.
     3→-- This migration enforces that every row is owned by an authenticated user.
     4→
     5→-- ── 1. Add user_id to quote_headings (missed in migration 004) ────────────────
     6→ALTER TABLE quote_headings
     7→  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS user_id UUID;
     8→
     9→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_quote_headings_user_id ON quote_headings(user_id);
    10→
    11→-- ── 2. Drop all existing "allow all" policies ─────────────────────────────────
    12→DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "allow all"               ON books;
    13→DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "allow all"               ON sessions;
    14→DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "allow all"               ON priority_items;
    15→DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "allow all"               ON book_notes;
    16→DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "allow all"               ON quotes;
    17→DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "allow_all_quote_headings" ON quote_headings;
    18→
    19→-- ── 3. User-scoped policies ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
    20→
    21→-- books
    22→CREATE POLICY "books_owner" ON books
    23→  FOR ALL
    24→  USING  (auth.uid() = user_id)
    25→  WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id);
    26→
    27→-- quotes
    28→CREATE POLICY "quotes_owner" ON quotes
    29→  FOR ALL
    30→  USING  (auth.uid() = user_id)
    31→  WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id);
    32→
    33→-- book_notes
    34→CREATE POLICY "book_notes_owner" ON book_notes
    35→  FOR ALL
    36→  USING  (auth.uid() = user_id)
    37→  WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id);
    38→
    39→-- connections
    40→CREATE POLICY "connections_owner" ON connections
    41→  FOR ALL
    42→  USING  (auth.uid() = user_id)
    43→  WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id);
    44→
    45→-- quote_headings
    46→CREATE POLICY "quote_headings_owner" ON quote_headings
    47→  FOR ALL
    48→  USING  (auth.uid() = user_id)
    49→  WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id);
    50→
    51→-- sessions + priority_items are unused by the app; keep open for now
    52→CREATE POLICY "allow all" ON sessions       FOR ALL USING (true) WITH CHECK (true);
    53→CREATE POLICY "allow all" ON priority_items FOR ALL USING (true) WITH CHECK (true);
    54→
    55→-- ── 4. Claim function (SECURITY DEFINER bypasses RLS) ────────────────────────
    56→-- Called once on first sign-in to assign all null-user_id rows to the new user.
    57→-- Idempotent: if the user already has data (count > 0), this is a no-op.
    58→
    59→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION claim_user_data(claiming_user_id UUID)
    60→RETURNS void
    61→LANGUAGE plpgsql
    62→SECURITY DEFINER
    63→SET search_path = public
    64→AS $$
    65→BEGIN
    66→  -- Only claim if this user has no existing books
    67→  IF (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM books WHERE user_id = claiming_user_id) = 0 THEN
    68→    UPDATE books          SET user_id = claiming_user_id WHERE user_id IS NULL;
    69→    UPDATE quotes         SET user_id = claiming_user_id WHERE user_id IS NULL;
    70→    UPDATE book_notes     SET user_id = claiming_user_id WHERE user_id IS NULL;
    71→    UPDATE connections    SET user_id = claiming_user_id WHERE user_id IS NULL;
    72→    UPDATE quote_headings SET user_id = claiming_user_id WHERE user_id IS NULL;
    73→  END IF;
    74→END;
    75→$$;
    76→
    77→-- Allow authenticated users to call this function
    78→GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION claim_user_data(UUID) TO authenticated;

    51→    .from('books')

1→-- Migration 006: Fix INSERT RLS on all user-scoped tables
     2→--
     3→-- Problem: the FOR ALL policy uses WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id), but
     4→-- API routes never pass user_id in INSERT payloads, so the column stays NULL
     5→-- and the check fails.
     6→--
     7→-- Fix: set DEFAULT auth.uid() on each user_id column so Postgres stamps it
     8→-- automatically on every INSERT. The existing WITH CHECK policy then passes
     9→-- because the default makes user_id equal to auth.uid() at insert time.

content.text:
Pushed. Here's a summary of everything that was built:

---

**Files created:**

| File | What it does |
|---|---|
| `supabase/migrations/007_add_subscription.sql` | Creates `profiles` table with `subscription_status`, `stripe_customer_id`, `is_comped`; trigger auto-creates a row on sign-up; backfills existing users |
| `lib/supabase-admin.ts` | Service-role Supabase client (bypasses RLS) for use in API routes |
| `app/api/stripe/checkout/route.ts` | POST — creates/reuses a Stripe customer, then creates a Checkout session for `monthly` or `annual` plan |
| `app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts` | POST — verifies Stripe signature, handles `checkout.session.completed`, `customer.subscription.updated`, `customer.subscription.deleted` |
| `app/api/stripe/portal/route.ts` | GET — creates a Stripe Customer Portal session so users can manage/cancel |
| `app/pricing/page.tsx` | Three-card pricing page: Basic (free), Pro ($4/mo, emphasized with amber border + "Most Popular" badge), Annual ($40/yr); Pro users see "Manage subscription" instead |

**Files modified:**

- `app/home/page.tsx` — fetches `profiles` on load; if free + 3+ active items, shows a lock icon on the `+` button and an "Upgrade for more" link
- `components/AddItemModal.tsx` — accepts `upgradeRequired` prop; when true, shows an upgrade prompt with a link to `/pricing` instead of the form

**Environment variables you still need to set:**
```
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY
NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET
STRIPE_MONTHLY_PRICE_ID
STRIPE_ANNUAL_PRICE_ID
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY   ← needed by the API routes
```
